title: CDP Runtime.evaluate Focus Guard: Pre-Minimize for Risky Expressions
desc: Runtime.evaluate with focus-risky expressions (.focus()/.click()/dispatchEvent etc) now triggers pre-minimize dance before CDP send. Also added to knownGuarded list so focus-theft reports as RACE not UNGUARDED.
steps: CdpClient.SendAsync: isFocusStealing now also true when method=='Runtime.evaluate' AND IsFocusRiskExpression(params.expression). IsFocusRiskExpression checks for .focus(), .click(), scrollIntoView(), requestSubmit(), dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent/MouseEvent), showOpenFilePicker.

When isFocusStealing=true: Chrome is pre-minimized (SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE=8) before CDP send and restored (SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE=4) after. Runtime.evaluate calls without focus-risky expressions skip this dance (no performance penalty).

AskCommands.Entry.Cdp.cs: knownGuarded array now includes 'Runtime.evaluate'. If focus-theft still fires after pre-minimize (race), it logs 'RACE' not 'UNGUARDED'. To add new focus-risky expressions, update IsFocusRiskExpression() in CdpClient.FocusRiskWatchdog.cs.
author: claude
